title: potentilla pennsylvanica subsp. hispanica
intro: Potentilla pennsylvanica subsp. hispanica, also known as the Shrubby Cinquefoil, is a deciduous shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its five-petaled pink or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Potentilla pennsylvanica subsp. hispanica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote overall well-being and treat various health conditions.
